questions.question: Consider a completely randomized design with 3 treatments (A, B, and C) and 5 replicates. If the total sum of squares (SST) is 150 and the sum of squares due to treatments (SSTt) is 60, find the sum of squares due to error (SSE).

A completely randomized design has 2 treatments (X and Y) with 6 replicates each. The total sum of squares (SST) is 240, the sum of squares due to treatments (SSTt) is 120, and the sum of squares due to error (SSE) is 60. What is the mean square due to error (MSE)?
In a completely randomized design, the sum of squares due to treatments (SSTt) is 10 times the sum of squares due to error (SSE). If the SST is 180, find the value of SSE. What is the value of SSTt?
